english: brush or pencil for artistic painting, Chinese writing; pir-togs(-pa) (pir togs (-pa)) painter Cs. - pir-doṅ (pir dong) receptacle or case for brushes. - pir-spu (pir spu) pencil-hair. - *pir-nyúg* (*pir nyug*) W. = bćad-pir (bcad pir); also for lead-pencil. - pir-śiṅ (pir shing) pencil-stick.
(spelling?) to crush, to grind (to powder) = mnyéd-pa (mnyed pa) Ld.
Ld. sieve.
v. pús-mo (pus mo).
acc. to Cs. Ssk., yet not to be found in Lexx., the wild fig-tree, Hindi: pipal.
num. figure for 73.
milfoil, (millefolium), yarrow; Lh.
(Ssk. पुत्री, daughter), a common female name (perh. bu-k̇rid (bu khrid).)
Glr. = pó-ti (po ti), book (perh. form-ed out of pústak (pustak)).
town in Bhotan.
v. spú-byi (spu byi).
a little rat-like animal, v. bra (bra) and zlum (zlum); pu-tse-śel (pu tse shel) prob. = pu-śel-tse (pu shel tse).
husks of barley W.; Cs. bran.
Mil., a district in Mṅa-ris (mnga' ris).
tube, any thing tubular and hollow, box of tin or wood, pen-case etc.; also = doṅ-po (dong po) the Tibetan shuttle; *pu-ri méd-k̇an* (*pu ri med mkhan*) W. full, solid, not hollow, cf. pá-ri (pa ri).
Ssk. man; soul; = skyés-bu (skyes bu).
Cs.: Ssk. masculine gender.
hut, built of stones, like those of the alpine herdsmen W., (Ts. rdzí-skyor (rdzi skyor)); kyi-pul (kyi pul) dog-kennel.
fence, Lex. = mda-yáb (mda' yab) and láṅ-kan (lang kan).
a medicinal herb Med.
shelf, partition in a box.
Pur. collar-bone.
C., W. an urn-shaped vessel of clay or wood, for water, beer etc. (seems not to be the same with búm-pa (bum pa)).
W., *pún-će* (*pun ce*) = lúd-će (lud ce) to run over.
Ssk., white lotos.
Cs. 1. steel-yard. - 2. púr-gyis (pur gyis) v. _opúr-ba ('pur ba). - 3. v. spur (spur).
v. pú-lu (pu lu).
Ssk. blue lotos.
Ssk. book.

W. *pís-mo* (*pis mo*), *píg-mo* (*pig mo*), knee; pús-mo sa-la _odzug-pa (pus mo sa la 'dzug pa) to kneel; *píg-mo tsúg-će* (*pig mo tsug ce*), *pí-tsúg gyáb-će* (*pi tsug gyab ce*) W. id.; *píg-mo tsúg-te dád-će* (*pig mo tsug te dad ce*) to sit in kneeling (which is considered indecorous); cf. tsog (tsog).
num. figure: 103.
also be-kár (be kar), pe-há-ra (pe ha ra), bi-hár (bi har) Lt., Glr., Mil., a much worshipped deity, v. kye-ṗáṅ (kye phang), and Schl. 157.
n. of a people Sch.
v. pa-ṇa (pa Na).
(Pers. پیوند), graft, scion; *pe-bán tsúg-će* (*pe ban tsug ce*) W. to graft.
little, small, a little Sch.
Chin. pai-tsái, Chinese white cabbage in C.; of late also known in Europe.
a flat basket.
